skill.name: command-optimization
skill.description: CLI command development specialist for designing, documenting, and improving command-line interfaces, argument handling, automation workflows, and help text.
skill.summary: Designs clear CLI commands with practical argument patterns, examples, validation, and documentation guidance.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns. Review confirmed a legitimate external command risk in the slash-command template and CLI examples, but the weak cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown text and command terminology, with no malicious intent or prompt injection found.

medium_findings.title: Slash Command Template Encourages Dynamic Shell Execution
medium_findings.description: The reference template includes dynamic shell context using the !`...` syntax and documents Bash tool allowances. This is a legitimate slash-command pattern, but generated commands could execute unsafe shell commands if authors fill placeholders with untrusted or destructive commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The dynamic shell syntax and Bash allowance examples are explicit. The risk depends on how placeholders are filled, so the concern is real but not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited high-risk weak cryptography alerts occur in Markdown descriptions, command examples, option text, and checklist content. I found no cryptographic implementation, weak hash usage, secret handling, or data exfiltration in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain plain Markdown prose, placeholders, or table content. No cryptographic APIs or algorithm names are used as executable code.

low_findings.title: CLI Command Examples Are Non-Executable Documentation
low_findings.description: Many static external-command matches are fenced examples for npm, lint, and migration commands. They are documentation samples rather than code that runs during skill installation or loading.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are inside Markdown code fences and illustrative examples. They do not execute automatically from SKILL.md.

dangerous_patterns.title: Auto-Executed Dynamic Context Placeholder
dangerous_patterns.description: The slash-command template uses !`{{SHELL_COMMAND_1}}` and !`{{SHELL_COMMAND_2}}`, which execute shell commands when a command loads in compatible environments. Authors should keep these commands read-only, scoped, and reviewed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The template explicitly uses auto-executed shell command placeholders. The placeholder form is not malicious by itself, but it is a meaningful execution surface.

content.value_statement: CLI commands often grow inconsistent, confusing, or unsafe to run. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code design clear command interfaces, argument patterns, validation, and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Designs task-oriented CLI command names, arguments, and options.
Improves existing command usability, defaults, feedback, and error messages.
Creates help text, usage examples, and README-ready command documentation.
Suggests validation checks for files, directories, configuration, and dependencies.
Provides slash-command template guidance with dynamic context and tool restrictions.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a complete CLI framework implementation.
Generated commands still need project-specific testing before production use.
Dynamic shell context must be reviewed before use in slash commands.
It does not verify permissions, dependencies, or runtime behavior automatically.

use_cases.title: Create Project Commands
use_cases.description: Design clear npm, script, or terminal commands for generators, migrations, tests, and build tasks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Tooling
use_cases.description: Create consistent naming, options, help text, and dry-run behavior across shared developer commands.

use_cases.title: Improve CLI Documentation
use_cases.description: Turn command behavior into concise usage guides, examples, option tables, and troubleshooting notes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Basic Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CLI command for [task]. Include the command name, required arguments, common options, usage examples, and error messages.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this command for usability: [command]. Improve naming, argument order, defaults, help text, validation, and output feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Command Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a consistent CLI command suite for [workflow]. Include command groups, shared flags, dry-run behavior, confirmation rules, and documentation structure.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Slash Command Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a slash-command template for [automation]. Include safe dynamic context, allowed tools, argument handling, workflow steps, and output format.

output_examples.input: Create a command to generate React components.
output_examples.output: A proposed generate:component command with component name input, path and test options, examples, validation rules, and concise help text.
A revised db:migrate interface with create, status, rollback, target-version options, safer defaults, dry-run guidance, and clearer user feedback.

output_examples.input: Document a new internal audit command.
output_examples.output: A usage section with purpose, arguments, flags, examples, output expectations, common errors, and troubleshooting guidance.

best_practices: Use action-oriented command names with predictable argument order and consistent flag names.
Add dry-run, verbose output, validation, and confirmations for risky operations.
Document examples, expected output, common errors, and recovery steps for each command.
anti_patterns: Avoid vague command names that hide the action or target object.
Avoid destructive defaults without confirmation, preview, or clear rollback guidance.
Avoid dynamic shell context that runs broad, writable, or unreviewed commands.

faq.question: Does this skill execute commands by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown guidance, but its slash-command template can describe commands that execute in compatible tools.

faq.question: Can it generate implementation code?
faq.answer: It can provide implementation sketches and command structure, but project owners must adapt and test the final code.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it useful outside JavaScript projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples use npm, but the command design patterns apply to many CLI ecosystems.

faq.question: What security review is needed before use?
faq.answer: Review generated shell commands, dynamic context, file writes, and destructive operations before enabling them.

faq.question: What is the main marketplace warning?
faq.answer: The slash-command template includes dynamic shell context, so generated commands should stay read-only unless explicitly approved.
